refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, focusing on communication latency as the critical factor for teleoperation safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and validate p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation trials.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link to maintain continuous teleoperation.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network infrastructure and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge computing modules near vehicle to reduce round-trip time.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data compression to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol optimization and network infrastructure.
Dependency on telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses latency reduction independently but requires coordination with network infrastructure managed elsewhere.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency testing.
Risk of protocol optimization conflicting with data integrity or safety alerts.
Risk that compression may add processing delay if not carefully implemented.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP3) if latency targets are not met by protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing communication protocols, data compression, and telemetry handling for teleoperation latency management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation commands and telemetry data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry feedback to the operator interface.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from operator or higher-level control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope, low containment specificity for latency optimization.
Physical vehicle module less suited for communication protocol logic.
High containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication and latency.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not implement latency-critical communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency-critical communication protocol optimization and telemetry handling are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Vehicle modules.
Risk of latency optimization conflicting with safety alert timing managed by Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services and Vehicle modules to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR sub-requirements with clear latency reduction focus and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if protocol optimization logic spreads across multiple modules, but containment in Teleoperation is strong.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows high containment score and low diffusion, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and network latency measurements is feasible within the Teleoperation module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related functionality to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface definitions between Teleoperation and Safety Services to ensure safety alert timing is not compromised.
Monitor potential latency impact of data compression algorithms during integration testing.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting architectural and safety constraints. Verification and ownership are well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link to maintain continuous teleoperation.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency constraints.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; current level meets requirements and architectural clarity.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed baseline during operation.
Latency measurement tools provide accurate telemetry data.
open_risks: Potential integration complexity with Safety Services for alert timing.
Compression algorithms may introduce processing delays if not optimized.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from teleoperation trials under real network conditions.
Interface compliance verification between Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
